note: vocative 'nāga' addressed to the Buddha/muni in praise (Taṁ namassāmi samecca nāga); honorific of the sage
narā nāgā ca gandhabbā, sabbe devā — nāgas in a list of beings coming to honour; passing mention
mahānāgaṁ devadevaṁ narāsabhaṁ — the great nāga = the Buddha approaching
nāgaṁ paggayha reṇūhi — offering an elephant (gift) sprinkled, alongside buddha-pūjā imagery
saṅgāmasīse nāgova bahū pāṇe viheṭhayaṁ — like an elephant in battle's front harming many; simile
āraññakā nāgā, kuñjarā... mātaṅgā īsādantā — forest elephants (kuñjara/mātaṅga/tusks); buddhanāgassa is epithet but dominant token is elephant
nāgova yūthāni vivajjayitvā, sañjātakhandho — Khaggavisāṇa: elephant leaving the herds; simile
varanāgo mayā dinno, īsādanto urūḷhavā, sahatthipo — a noble elephant given as gift (tusks, with mahout)
tosetvāna mahānāgaṁ, alatthaṁ upasampadaṁ — pleasing the great nāga (Buddha/teacher), received ordination
nāgaseṭṭho mayā dinno īsādanto urūḷhavā; nāgadānaṁ — gift of a fine elephant (tusks); Hatthidāyaka (elephant-giver)
balī nāgo upagantvā yūthā nīharate gajaṁ — strong elephant drives a tusker from the herd (mātaṅga/kuñjara/gaja); nāgariko here is 'citizen' but dominant nāga token is elephant
manussā devā nāgā ca / nāgā ca garuḷā — nāgas in a list of beings building a stūpa; passing mention
Nāgakesariya thera — the elder's proper name (apadāna colophon); built on nāga- as personal name
tuvaranāganaḷinā in an uddāna list of flower/plant offering-titles; nāga as ironwood/plant
dvipadindaṁ mahānāgaṁ lokajeṭṭhaṁ narāsabhaṁ — the great nāga = the Buddha (lord of bipeds)
nāgapunnāgaketakā, punnāgā giripunnāgā — flowering trees in an assame flora list (Sāriputta); mahānāgaṁ/nāgo elsewhere but dominant prov is tree
rathena nāgayuttena upesi — king came by a chariot yoked with an elephant; royal elephant
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā viharāmi anāsavo — stock simile: elephant breaking its tether
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant-breaking-bonds simile; punnāga is the tree but the nāga token is the elephant simile

punāgamiṁ = puna + āgamiṁ ('I came back'); not the nāga morpheme, sandhi artefact
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nānāgandhe = nānā + gandhe ('various scents'); nāg is a sandhi artefact, not 'nāga'

nānāgandhavanantare = nānā-gandha-vana ('grove of various scents'); sandhi artefact
campakaṁ nāgapupphañca — nāga-flower (ironwood blossom) in a flower-offering list; hatthināge/nāgova also present but dominant prov is tree/flower
nāgūpamo mahāvīro / buddhanāgo asādiso — nāga-similes (with sīha/usabha/sakka) praising the Buddha; honorific
vanditvāna mahānāgaṁ ... Sumedhaṁ lokanāyakaṁ — the great nāga = the Buddha (lokanāyaka) being honoured
nānāgandhasameritā = nānā-gandha-sameritā ('wafting various scents'); sandhi artefact

claim: nāgas and garuḷas come to the Buddha desiring to hear Dhamma
note: nāgā ca garuḷā ubho, dhammañca sotukāmā āgacchuṁ buddhasantikaṁ — nāgas as beings who come to hear Dhamma
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
punnāgā giripunnāgā, mahānāgā — flowering trees in a jātassara/assame flora list; nānāgandha- is sandhi but dominant prov is tree
saṭṭhi nāgasahassāni... suvaṇṇakacchā mātaṅgā, tomaraṅkusapāṇibhi — 60,000 elephants with mahouts/goads (mātaṅga); royal retinue
saṭṭhi nāgasahassāni... suvaṇṇakacche mātaṅge, saṅgāmāvacare; nāgavanike — war-elephants and elephant-forest; royal elephants
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
punnāgā giripunnāgā... pupphitā — flowering trees in an assame flora list; nāgā is part of the tree/flower enumeration
saṭṭhi nāgasahassāni... suvaṇṇakacchā mātaṅgā, tehi nāgehi parivuto; nāgova — herd of caparisoned elephants with mahouts; nāgova too is elephant simile
nāgā sālā ca saḷalā, puṇḍarīka... pupphitā — nāga(-tree) in a flowering-tree assame list
pūjayiṁ nāgakesaraṁ — offered nāga-kesara (ironwood) flowers; tree/flower
nāgapallavamādāya buddhassa abhiropayiṁ — took nāga(-tree) shoots/sprigs and offered; ironwood foliage offering
Nāgapallaviko in an uddāna list of elder-names (the Nāgapallava thera); proper name in colophon

upāgataṁ mahānāgaṁ jalajuttamanāmakaṁ — the great nāga = the Buddha (Padumuttara) who approached
nānāgandhe = nānā-gandhe ('various scents'); sandhi artefact
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
samādhiṁsu mahānāgaṁ, sissā ācariyaṁ yathā — honoured the great nāga (Buddha) as pupils their teacher
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nānāgandhasamīritā = nānā-gandha-samīritā ('wafting various scents'); sandhi artefact
gahetvā giripunnāgaṁ ... abhipūjayiṁ — took giri-punnāga (ironwood) flowers and offered; tree/flower (Giripunnāgiya thera)

Sakyaputto mahānāgo, aggo loke sadevake — the great nāga = the future Buddha (Sakyan son), supreme in the world

claim: long-lived nāgas, possessed of iddhi and great renown, come under the donor's sway
note: dīghāyukā nāgā, iddhimanto mahāyasā — nāgas characterised as long-lived, powerful, in a list of beings (with supaṇṇas, sīhas)
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
dhorayho usabho nāgo, sīho sakko — nāga among epithets (bull, lion, Sakka) of the Buddha; honorific
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nāgavikkantagamano (sīhahanu, haṁsadundubhinissano) — 'with the gait of a nāga/elephant', mark of the Buddha Padumuttara; honorific descriptor
manujāmaranāgānaṁ, brahmānañca samāgame — in the assembly of men, gods, nāgas and brahmas; nāgas in a list of beings, passing
sadevāsuranāgehi manujehi purakkhato — attended by devas, asuras, nāgas, men; nāgas in a list of beings
punāgamaṁ = puna + āgamaṁ ('I came back to the forest'); sandhi artefact
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
ākāsenāgamā = ākāsena + āgamā ('came through the air'); sandhi artefact
manussā devanāgā ca / nāgā ca garuḷā — men, devas, nāgas (and garuḷas) honouring the stūpa; list of beings
varanāgo mayā dinno, īsādanto urūḷhavā, sahatthipo — a noble elephant given (tusks, with mahout)
nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā — elephant breaking tether simile
nāgasatasahassāni tidhāpabhinnā mātaṅgā; āruyha sirikaṁ nāgaṁ; nāgo nuddharate padaṁ — riding the royal elephant in rut (mātaṅga, three-fold-in-rut); the rutting elephant won't lift its foot

bhittikhīle nāgadante — 'elephant-tusk' wall-pegs (ivory hooks); nāgadanta = ivory pin. nāgasamāla/nāgasamālo is the elder's name in the vagga title, but the contextual token nāgadante is elephant(ivory)
nibbutattaṁ mahānāgaṁ, devasaṅghanamassitaṁ — the great nāga = Buddha Siddhattha, quenched, honoured by the host of devas
anāgārehi = an-agāra ('homeless ones'); sandhi/prefix artefact, not 'nāga'
nāgo saṅgāmasīseva, sato tatrādhivāsaye — like an elephant at the battlefront, one should endure; elephant simile
nāgo saṅgāmasīseva — elephant-at-battlefront simile (Aññāsikoṇḍañña)
explicit folk-etymology pun: 'na hi āguṁ karoti so' (he does no wrong) — the nāga = the arahant/Buddha; sabbesaṁ nāganāmānaṁ saccanāmo; honorific. polysemy true (puns serpent/elephant on a human)
bhikkhavo tisatā... nāgā vandantu satthuno — the (300) bhikkhus called nāgā, honorific of arahants paying homage; Sela's verse
Chaddanta context: nāgassāsi (the elephant's), gajo, chaddanto — the six-tusked elephant who speaks the kāsāva verse; elephant
Sunāga thera — the elder's proper name (Sunāgattheragāthā, 'Sunāgo thero')
sunāgo nāgito — elder-names in the uddāna (Sunāga and Nāgita theras); proper names
nāgaṁva thambhamhi daḷhāya rajjuyā — binding the mind like an elephant to a post with a firm rope (Tālapuṭa); elephant simile
nāgo yathā pūtilataṁva bandhanaṁ — like an elephant snapping a rotten-creeper bond (Moggallāna); elephant simile
nāgaṁ nāgassa orasaṁ — 'the nāga, true son of the nāga' (Rāhula son of the Buddha); honorific of person; nāganāmo also of the teacher
nāgo saṅgāmasīseva, sato tatrādhivāsaye — elephant-at-battlefront simile (Yasoja)
mahānāgaṁ mahāvīraṁ mahājutimanāsavaṁ — the great nāga = the Buddha (the seer Senaka beheld); honorific
Mahānāgo ca kullo ca — in the uddāna list of elder-names (the elder Mahānāga); proper name
Mahānāgattheragāthā, 'Mahānāgo thero' — the verses are of the elder named Mahānāga; proper name of the speaker
nāgīva bandhanaṁ chetvā viharāmi anāsavā — like a she-elephant breaking its bond; the elephant-breaking-tether simile (feminine)
nāgāsurā ca brahmāno / sanāgāsurabrahmakā — nāgas, asuras and brahmas honouring Gotamī's parinibbāna; list of beings. (nāgīva refrain is elephant but dominant token here is the nāga-class being-list)

claim: a nāga-king hosts and feeds the Buddha in the nāga realm and a nāga-maiden makes the anumodanā
note: mahoragassa bhavane, nisīdi paramāsane; nāgarājā mahāyasaṁ; nāgakaññā mahiddhikā — nāga-king's realm, food-offering, powerful nāga-maiden; serpent-beings as devout, capable hosts
nāgīva bandhanaṁ chetvā — she-elephant breaking tether simile
nāgīva bandhanaṁ chetvā — she-elephant breaking tether simile
nāgīva bandhanaṁ chetvā — she-elephant breaking tether simile
devā nāgā ca kinnarā / devā ca kinnarā nāgā — nāgas in a list of beings born in past/future assemblies; passing mention
nāgīva bandhanaṁ chetvā — she-elephant breaking tether simile
nāgā ca susumārā ca, ye caññe udake carā — nāgas and crocodiles and other water-dwellers; the water-creature listing (Puṇṇā's ritual-bathing satire) treats nāga as an aquatic being, passing mention
nāgabhogasadisopamā ubho ūrū — 'both thighs like a nāga's coils/body' (nāga-bhoga = serpent's coil); simile vehicle drawing on the serpent's coiled body
nāgo chetvāva bandhanaṁ — '(they go forth) like an elephant having cut its bond'; elephant-breaking-tether simile

nāgaṁ ogāhamuttiṇṇaṁ nadītīramhi; puriso aṅkusamādāya; nāgo pasārayī pādaṁ — an elephant at a river bank, a man with a goad, the elephant extends its foot and is mounted (Dantikā's tamed-elephant simile)
...dantaṁ guttaṁ yatindriyaṁ nāgaṁ — the Buddha described (tamed, guarded, restrained) as 'nāga'; honorific of the Buddha (Bāhiya)
yakkho nāgena pañcamaṁ — uddāna mnemonic; the 'nāga' is the elder/great-one of the Yakkhapahāra sutta (Sāriputta as mahānāga). honorific in the chapter-summary
tesaṁ ubhinnaṁ mahānāgānaṁ — 'those two great nāgas' = Sāriputta and Moggallāna (the two arahants); honorific of persons
hatthināgo... hatthīhi hatthinīhi hatthikalabhehi; soṇḍā/tiṇa — the Pārileyya bull-elephant crowded by the herd; literal elephant (Nāgasutta)

claim: the great ocean is the dwelling of mighty beings including nāgas, with bodies up to hundreds of yojanas
note: mahāsamuddo mahataṁ bhūtānaṁ āvāso... asurā nāgā gandhabbā; yojanasatikā attabhāvā — nāgas as great ocean-dwelling beings of huge size
...dantaṁ guttaṁ yatindriyaṁ nāgaṁ — the Buddha described as 'nāga' (same formula as Bāhiya); honorific of the Buddha (Soṇa)
sataṁ hemavatā nāgā, īsādantā urūḷhavā, suvaṇṇakacchā mātaṅgā — a hundred Himalayan elephants with tusks (mātaṅga); literal elephants in a wealth-comparison
abhiruyha gajavaraṁ; nāgassa dantesu duvesu nimmitā — mounting the excellent elephant; lotuses created on the elephant's two tusks (Nāga-vimāna = the elephant-vehicle vimāna)
sataṁ hemavatā nāgā, īsādantā... mātaṅgā — a hundred Himalayan tusker-elephants; wealth-comparison (parallel to vv20)
tassa nāgassa khandhamhi (vāraṇo, gacchati) — on the shoulder of that elephant; the Kuñjara(elephant)-vimāna; literal elephant
Mañjiṭṭhā pabhassarā nāgā — uddāna of vimāna-titles; 'Nāgā' = the Nāga(-elephant)vimāna entries (vv41/vv60-62 are elephant-vehicle vimānas); elephant
susukkakhandhaṁ abhiruyha nāgaṁ... dantiṁ; abhiruyha gajavaraṁ; nāgassa dantesu — mounting a white tusker (danti/gajavara); the elephant-vehicle vimāna
mahantaṁ nāgaṁ abhiruyha, sabbasetaṁ gajuttamaṁ — mounting a great all-white supreme elephant (gajuttama); elephant-vehicle vimāna
dibbena yānena, sabbasetena hatthinā — divine vehicle, an all-white elephant (hatthi); the third elephant-vimāna
rathā ca nāgā tūriyāni ca — chariots and elephants and instruments in a vimāna pleasure-scene; elephants
tenāgamā = tena + āgamā ('thereby came'); the bhikkhu Sāriputta came; sandhi artefact, not 'nāga'
